D2 Vision L2 Understand Atom

Fovea vs Periphery Acuity

see why only a thumbnail-sized patch of your vision is actually sharp

Cones crowd the fovea and thin out toward the edge, so resolvable detail collapses with eccentricity. Peripheral vision is great for motion, poor for fine pattern.

A row of gratings sits at growing eccentricity from the fixation cross. Set the pattern's fineness and the highlighted eccentricity; watch peripheral patches wash out as the acuity curve falls.
